name: Dataset'25
desc: 36 hours, 3rd place at a national hackathon by ECDS & VIT Alumni, sponsored by KVB and ADRIG AI. Led Team DataDrive in building a dual-pipeline multilingual speech assessment system fine-tuned Whisper ASR on synthetic slurring data, phoneme-level error analysis, prosody & disfluency detection, and an LLM layer generating clinician-grade reports and parent-friendly summaries.

name: Hack4Health
desc: Two rounds, two podium finishes. Round 1 presented and placed at IAIC 2025 (SpoRIC, VIT Chennai, Aug 28–29), getting shortlisted for Round 2. Round 2, 2nd place at the full MedTech hackathon by SCOPE in collaboration with Perfint Healthcare, V-NEST, and IIC (Sep 11–12). Designed the complete software pipeline, handled real-time data ingestion, built and validated ML models, and integrated software with hardware for live breath-hold detection.

name: Smart India Hackathon
desc: Shortlisted top 50 out of 800+ teams across India for the national stage. Led system and pipeline design for AgroBot, a semi-automated pesticide rover using Raspberry Pi and computer vision for targeted plant infection detection, reducing chemical waste through precision spraying.

name: YUVA AI Thon
desc: 24-hour hackathon by YUVA Club, VIT Chennai, in collaboration with Young Indians and IBM. Built a multi-agent hybrid agentic AI system where domain-specific agents (feasibility, design, resources) collaborate under a master orchestrator to convert abstract ideas into step-by-step execution pipelines, PPT decks, and clinical-grade documentation.

name: GlitchCon 2025
desc: 24-hour hackathon by VIT Alumni Association. First deep dive into the financial domain and agentic AI, built a personalized financial advisory system that analyses users' bank statement spending histories to generate contextual, actionable financial guidance. Sparked an ongoing interest in AI for finance.

name: MedHack 2025
desc: Crossed to the other side of the table. Served as Student Coordinator for MEDHACK at VIT Chennai, managing registrations, providing technical support to teams on medical image processing challenges, and handling real-time logistics. Witnessing participants shape ideas into working solutions in real time reaffirmed why hackathons are such powerful learning environments.

name: REVIVE
desc: Where it all started. Built and pitched an unmanned rover concept for assisting people trapped under rubble after disasters, made in collaboration with REVIVE, Humano1dX, and AutoVIT. The idea was ambitious, the execution nascent, but the experience introduced image processing and CAD design, igniting interests that define my work today.
